steps.title: Introduce novel and author
steps.content: The student wants to introduce Paradise to readers unfamiliar with both the novel and Salim Baraka. A strong sentence should tell the audience what the book is about and also provide author context that helps readers understand why Baraka is notable.

steps.title: Keep only relevant details
steps.content: The most relevant notes are that Paradise is a much-praised 1994 historical novel about colonial East Africa and that Baraka won the 2021 Nobel Prize in Literature. Details about where Baraka was born or currently lives are less useful for introducing both the novel and its author.

steps.title: Reject incomplete introductions
steps.content: Choice A centers on Baraka's biography and Nobel Prize but never explains what Paradise is about. Choice B identifies the novel's subject and praise, but it leaves out the Nobel credential that would introduce the author to unfamiliar readers. Choice D describes the novel's setting but gives little author context and adds a homeland claim not supported by the notes. Choice C combines the novel's genre, subject, year, praise, and Baraka's Nobel credential in one focused introduction. The answer is C.
